name: GPTScript
what_it_does: Natural language scripting framework where you write AI workflows in .gpt files using plain English, and GPTScript executes them by calling LLMs, tools, and external APIs.
use_cases: Automate multi-step workflows (file manipulation, web browsing, API calls) defined entirely in natural language scripts
Build agent pipelines that chain LLM calls and shell tools without writing Python glue code
Create reusable AI tools shareable as plain text .gpt files checked into version control
Orchestrate AI assistants that can invoke HTTP endpoints and parse structured responses
Rapid prototyping of agentic workflows where the script itself serves as documentation
not_for: Complex stateful agent memory or long-horizon planning — GPTScript is script-oriented, not a full agent runtime
Teams that need type-safe structured output validation with Pydantic or JSON Schema enforcement
Production systems requiring fine-grained error handling and retry logic in code
best_when: You want to define an AI workflow as a readable, versionable text file that non-Python developers can understand and modify.
avoid_when: You need tight programmatic control over agent state, memory, or complex branching logic in code.

auth.notes: LLM provider API keys (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.) passed via environment variables. No GPTScript-specific auth.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Unlimited — costs come from LLM provider API usage only
pricing.notes: Apache 2.0 licensed. You pay your LLM provider (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.) directly.

performance.rate_limits: None — local runtime; rate limits come from the underlying LLM provider

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Script parse errors are reasonably clear; LLM tool-call failures bubble up with provider error messages
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: LLM generation is non-deterministic; script steps are not transactional
known_agent_gotchas: Tools are YAML-like .gpt script blocks, not Python functions — the mental model differs from most Python agent frameworks
Debugging is difficult — no step-through debugger; rely on --verbose flag and print statements
Context window management is implicit; long scripts with many tool calls can silently truncate history
Tool sharing and imports use a URL-based system that requires network access at runtime
SDKs (Python, Node) are thinner wrappers around the Go CLI binary — the binary must be installed separately

security_score_components.security_notes: Scripts can execute arbitrary shell commands — review .gpt files before running untrusted scripts. API keys via env vars; never embed in .gpt files.
